ogTitle: Withers vs Wittenoom Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Withers and Wittenoom Hills.
heading: Withers vs Wittenoom Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Withers, Western Australia and Wittenoom Hills, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Withers has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Wittenoom Hills (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Withers or Wittenoom Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Withers has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Wittenoom Hills scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Withers scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Wittenoom Hills scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Withers or Wittenoom Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Withers has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
